creator: polyduekes@proton.me
text:
i would like to comment that this bug affects me as well

gdb /usr/local/bin/ncdu2 output :-

(gdb) run
Starting program: /usr/local/bin/ncdu2 

Program received signal SIGILL, Illegal instruction.
Privileged opcode.
0x0000000001095ca2 in ?? ()
(gdb) bt
#0  0x0000000001095ca2 in ?? ()
#1  0x0000000001082d60 in ?? ()
#2  0x0000000001079c6f in ?? ()
#3  0x0000000001070bed in ?? ()
#4  0x00000000010709a7 in ?? ()
#5  0x000000080136dc3a in __libc_start1 (argc=1, argv=0x7fffffffeb00, 
    env=0x7fffffffeb10, cleanup=<optimized out>, mainX=0x1070830)
    at /usr/src/lib/libc/csu/libc_start1.c:157
#6  0x00000000010707ad in ?? ()
(gdb) x/4i 0x0000000001095ca2
=> 0x1095ca2:	vmovdqu (%rax,%rcx,1),%ymm0
   0x1095ca7:	vptestnmb %ymm0,%ymm0,%k0
   0x1095cad:	kmovd  %k0,-0x54(%rbp)
   0x1095cb3:	kmovd  %k0,%eax


cpu info as per dmesg:-

CPU: Intel(R) Core(TM) i5 CPU         650  @ 3.20GHz (3191.27-MHz K8-class CPU)
  Origin="GenuineIntel"  Id=0x20652  Family=0x6  Model=0x25  Stepping=2
  Features=0xbfebfbff<FPU,VME,DE,PSE,TSC,MSR,PAE,MCE,CX8,APIC,SEP,MTRR,PGE,MCA,CMOV,PAT,PSE36,CLFLUSH,DTS,ACPI,MMX,FXSR,SSE,SSE2,SS,HTT,TM,PBE>
  Features2=0x298e3ff<SSE3,PCLMULQDQ,DTES64,MON,DS_CPL,VMX,SMX,EST,TM2,SSSE3,CX16,xTPR,PDCM,SSE4.1,SSE4.2,POPCNT,AESNI>
  AMD Features=0x28100800<SYSCALL,NX,RDTSCP,LM>
  AMD Features2=0x1<LAHF>
  VT-x: PAT,HLT,MTF,PAUSE,EPT,UG,VPID
  TSC: P-state invariant, performance statistics

freebsd-version     
14.2-RELEASE-p2

pkg info | fgrep ncdu2
ncdu2-2.7                      NCurses Disk Usage (stable version written in Zig)

i would also agree that the binaries on freebsd ports repo should be generic rather then optimised for a specific subset
